title: Multiply by 10
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic A: The properties of multiplication and division

assessment_items.question_content:
**Multiply.**  
*Hint: Use the array below to help solve the equation.*

$10 \times 2 = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

![A rectangular array with 10 rows with 2 dots per row](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/610a112e9a710e9dec004e3100f724f22f894a08)
hints:
The array has $2$ columns with $10$ dots per column.

We can find the total number of dots by counting by $10$s.

![The same array with the columns partially numbered by tens](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b39f10323d46d6b986b123ca627713d499df2eba)

There are $\maroonD{20}$ total dots.

![The same array with the columns numbered 10, 20](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e09eb63b03bd61b75856d9a6a1199d273e52a3f4)
